trigger_sources: RiskRegisterEngine after create_risk() completes (currently not emitted)

current_state.summary: PLACEHOLDER — the handler body is a single logger.debug() call and a comment: 'Placeholder: future implementation will trigger scorecard recalculation'. No engine is called. No action is taken. The event is registered on both string keys (RISK_ASSESSED and risk.assessed) on the TrustGraph bus but the handler does nothing.
current_state.intended_purpose: Trigger SecurityHealthScorecard or SecurityPostureScoring recalculation when any risk is assessed or updated.
current_state.comment_in_code: # Placeholder: future implementation will trigger scorecard recalculation

what_works: Event registration is in place — RISK_ASSESSED events are received by the subscriber.
org_id is extracted from event payload.
logger.debug() at least produces a trace-level log entry indicating the event was received.
_safe_call is not needed (no downstream call) — no failure risk.
what_doesnt: THE ENTIRE HANDLER IS A PLACEHOLDER — no engine is called, no action is taken when risk is assessed.
SecurityHealthScorecardEngine.create_snapshot() is not called despite being the stated intent.
SecurityPostureScoringEngine is not notified — posture scores are never recalculated in response to risk changes.
RiskAggregatorEngine composite grade is not recomputed when individual risks are assessed.
RISK_ASSESSED is emitted by on_threat_detected() (as a best-effort risk entry) but receives no downstream processing.
RISK_ASSESSED is missing from legacy EventBus type_map.
No test covers this subscriber (trivially passes as it does nothing).
The entire purpose of this event — connecting risk assessment to posture scoring — is unimplemented, leaving a critical intelligence gap in the platform.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given RISK_ASSESSED with org_id='acme', SecurityHealthScorecardEngine.create_snapshot() is called with org_id='acme' to recompute the scorecard.
AC2: Given RISK_ASSESSED, SecurityPostureScoringEngine records a new posture snapshot with the updated risk data.
AC3: Given RISK_ASSESSED with risk_level='critical', RiskAggregatorEngine.compute_org_score() is triggered to recompute the composite A-F grade.
AC4: Given RISK_ASSESSED with a risk_id in the payload, the risk_id must be stored in the posture snapshot for audit linkage.
AC5: RISK_ASSESSED must be added to the legacy EventBus type_map.
AC6: All downstream calls must be wrapped in separate _safe_call() wrappers so one failure does not prevent others.
AC7: The placeholder comment and logger.debug() must be replaced with real implementation — no placeholder code in production.
AC8: A pytest test verifies: RISK_ASSESSED event → SecurityHealthScorecardEngine called, SecurityPostureScoringEngine called, RiskAggregatorEngine called for critical risk.

AC9: Rate-limiting: if RISK_ASSESSED fires more than 10 times per minute for the same org_id, posture recalculation must be debounced (coalesced to one recalculation per minute) to prevent score thrashing under bulk risk ingestion.
